        "contents": "# Cursor ↔ Testing Agent Workflow (Semi-Automated)\n\n## 🎯 Current Setup (80% Automated)\n\n### **What's Automated:**\n1. ✅ Cursor pushes → Git hook auto-triggers\n2. ✅ Signal file created with commit SHA\n3. ✅ Watcher detects and creates test trigger\n4. ✅ Testing agent runs Playwright\n5. ✅ Results written to JSON\n6. ✅ Cursor reads results next session\n\n### **What's Manual:**\n- ❌ Ben needs to tell testing agent \"check for triggers\"\n- (This is the only manual step)\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start (5 Minutes)\n\n### **Step 1: Start the Watcher (Terminal 1)**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncd /Users/bencrowe/Development/apr-dashboard-v3\n./.cursor/watch-for-cursor.sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nLeave this running. You'll see:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n🤖 Claude Code Testing Agent Started\n👀 Watching for: .cursor/testing-needed.txt\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### **Step 2: When You See \"Signal detected!\"**\n\nThe watcher will show:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n🔔 Signal detected! Commit: abc123\n✅ Test trigger created: .cursor/test-trigger.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### **Step 3: Tell Your Testing Agent**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"Check for test triggers and run verification\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### **Step 4: Testing Agent Automatically:**\n- Reads &#96;.cursor/test-trigger.json&#96;\n- Runs the Playwright tests listed\n- Writes &#96;.cursor/test-results.json&#96;\n\n### **Step 5: Cursor (Me) Next Session:**\n- Reads &#96;.cursor/test-results.json&#96;\n- Sees what passed/failed\n- Auto-fixes failures\n- Pushes again → Cycle repeats\n\n---\n\n## 📋 For Ben: Your Only Manual Step\n\n**When watcher shows \"Signal detected!\":**\n\nOption A (Quick):\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nTell testing agent: \"Run verification tests\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOption B (Specific):\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nTell testing agent: \"Check .cursor/test-trigger.json and run those tests\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThat's it! Everything else is automated.\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 The Complete Flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n┌─────────────┐\n│   Cursor    │ 1. Makes fixes\n│             │ 2. git push\n└──────┬──────┘\n       │ AUTOMATED ✅\n       ▼\n┌─────────────────┐\n│   Git Hook      │ 3. Creates signal\n│   (post-push)   │",

        "contents": "# API Testing Methodology - Empirical Integration Testing Guide\n\n**Version:** 1.1\n**Created:** November 16, 2025\n**Updated:** November 16, 2025\n**Status:** ✅ Production-Ready Methodology\n**Applies To:** Any API-to-API integration project\n\n---\n\n## ⚡ STEP 0: THE FOUNDATIONAL PRINCIPLE (START HERE)\n\n&gt; **🎯 CRITICAL: This is the FIRST step for ANY API integration. Do this BEFORE any testing scripts, BEFORE any analysis, BEFORE any debugging.**\n\n### The Dead Simple Approach That Works 90% of the Time\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser fills ALL fields in target app → Gives agent record ID → Agent queries it → Done\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**That's it. The API shows you the answer. Just look at it.**\n\n---\n\n### 🧑 USER RESPONSIBILITIES (User: Read This First!)\n\n**⚠️ USER: Before asking the agent to debug or test ANY API integration:**\n\n1. **Create ONE complete test record** in the target application (Valcre, ClickUp, Stripe, etc.)\n   - Fill in **ALL fields** you want to map\n   - Use realistic test data\n   - Don't skip any fields\n\n2. **Note the record ID** (job number, task ID, record number, etc.)\n   - Example: VAL251031, TASK-12345, cust_abc123\n\n3. **Give the agent:**\n   - The record ID\n   - API credentials (if not already configured)\n   - Access to query the API\n\n**That's it. The agent can now see EXACTLY what the API expects.**\n\n---\n\n### 🤖 AGENT RESPONSIBILITIES\n\n**When user provides a complete test record ID:**\n\n1. **Authenticate** with the target API\n2. **Query the exact record** by ID\n3. **Analyze the returned JSON** - this shows you:\n   - Exact field names\n   - Exact data formats\n   - Exact enum values\n   - Data types\n   - Null vs populated fields\n4. **Map fields directly** from what you see\n5. **Done** - no guessing required\n\n---\n\n### Why This Works (It's Almost Too Simple)\n\n**The Insight:**\n- Any app with API access will return its own data structure\n- If you fill in a record and query it, **the API tells you the answer**\n- No documentation needed\n- No guessing\n- No trial and error\n\n**Example - Valcre Multi-Family Type:**\n\n**❌ What We Did (The Hard Way):**",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "# ClickUp 4-Stage Automation Testing Guide\n\nQuick reference for testing the complete ClickUp task lifecycle automation.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Make script executable\nchmod +x test-clickup-4-stages.sh\n\n# Test all stages interactively\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh all\n\n# Test specific stage only\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh 1    # Stage 1 only\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh 2    # Stages 1-2\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh 2.5  # Stages 1-2.5\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh 3    # All stages 1-3\n\n# Cleanup test data\n./test-clickup-4-stages.sh cleanup\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## What Each Test Does\n\n### Stage 1: Form Submission → Create ClickUp Task\n**Simulates:** Client submitting appraisal request form\n\n**Actions:**\n1. Creates test job in &#96;job_submissions&#96; table\n2. Calls &#96;create-clickup-task&#96; Edge Function\n3. ClickUp task created with Stage 1 format\n\n**Expected Result:**\n- Task name: &#96;PENDING - Test Plaza, 123 Test Lane&#96;\n- Description has client info, property info, submission notes\n- Bottom shows: &#96;⏳ Waiting for LOE Quote Preparation...&#96;\n- Links at top: APR Dashboard, Coming Soon placeholders\n\n---\n\n### Stage 2: Valcre Job Created → Update ClickUp Task\n**Simulates:** Appraiser creating Valcre job after filling LOE details\n\n**Actions:**\n1. Creates LOE details in &#96;job_loe_details&#96; table\n2. Calls &#96;update-clickup-task&#96; Edge Function\n3. Task updated with LOE section\n\n**Expected Result:**\n- Task name updated: &#96;VAL251999 - Test Plaza, 123 Test Lane&#96;\n- LOE section added with:\n  - 📋 LOE QUOTE PREPARED header\n  - Job Details (VAL number, date)\n  - Property Valuation (Rights, Scope, Report Type)\n  - Financial Details (Fee, Retainer, Terms)\n  - Comments (General, Delivery, Payment)\n- Valcre Job link updated from \"Coming Soon\" to actual link\n- Stage 1 data preserved\n\n---\n\n### Stage 2.5: DocuSeal Send → Add \"LOE Sent\" Timestamp\n**Simulates:** DocuSeal sending LOE for signature\n\n**Actions:**\n1. Sends DocuSeal webhook with &#96;submission.created&#96; event\n2. &#96;docuseal-webhook&#96; function adds timestamp\n3. Task updated with \"LOE Sent\" line\n\n**Expected Result:**",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/APR-Domain Mgr/architecture-v4/APR-Platform-Strategy.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {

        "contents": "# APR Dashboard v3 - Platform Independence Strategy\n\n&gt; **Objective:** Eliminate Valcre, ClickUp, and Pipedrive dependencies. Build a complete appraisal business platform on owned infrastructure.\n\n---\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nAPR Dashboard v3 is evolving from a \"report builder with integrations\" to a **complete appraisal business platform**. The goal is platform independence - owning the full stack rather than stitching together SaaS tools that each take 10% of their value while adding complexity.\n\n### Target State\n\n| Function | Current | Target | Status |\n|----------|---------|--------|--------|\n| Report Generation | Valcre | Report Builder | ~80% complete |\n| Project Management | ClickUp | Job Mgt v3 | ~60% complete |\n| CRM / Pipeline | Pipedrive | Acquisition Pipeline | ~40% complete |\n| E-Signatures | DocuSeal | DocuSeal (keep) | ✅ Complete |\n| Database | Supabase | Supabase (keep) | ✅ Complete |\n| File Storage | Google Drive | Supabase Storage (optional migrate) | ✅ Working |\n\n### Tech Stack Simplification\n\n**Before:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSupabase + Valcre + ClickUp + Pipedrive + DocuSeal + Google Drive\n    ↓         ↓         ↓          ↓          ↓           ↓\n  Core    Reports    Tasks      CRM      E-Sign      Files\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**After:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSupabase + DocuSeal\n    ↓          ↓\nEverything   E-Sign\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Three Workstreams\n\n### Workstream A: Report Builder (Kill Valcre)\n\n**Goal:** Complete Valcre replacement with specialized section editors\n\n**Already Done:**\n- ✅ Field registry (944 fields mapped)\n- ✅ Calculator engine (277 metrics, validated exact match)\n- ✅ HTML template system (70+ pages)\n- ✅ Live preview with real-time updates\n- ✅ Income Approach panel (3-panel: input → calc → preview)\n- ✅ Operating History panel\n- ✅ Sales Comparison panel\n- ✅ Value Reconciliation panel\n- ✅ PostMessage bridge (500+ fields flowing)\n\n**Remaining:**\n- ⏳ Home Tab (workbook setup, common fields)\n- ⏳ Image Manager (grid, reorder, layout picker)\n- ⏳ Narrative Editor (rich text, templates)\n- ⏳ Approach Toggles (show/hide Income/Sales/Cost)",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/15-Contract-review/3-Calc Eng &amp; Plan Agent/-Planning-Notes-Calculator-Demo.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 63

        "contents": "# Planning Notes: Calculator Demo Page\n\n**Status:** 📋 PLANNING COMPLETE - Ready for Implementation\n**Date:** 2025-12-12\n**Project:** APR Dashboard V3\n**Focus:** Interactive calculator demonstration page with calculation transparency\n\n---\n\n## 🔍 QUICK REFERENCE\n\n**When resuming work in this area, READ FIRST:**\n\n1. **Full Implementation Plan:** &#96;Calculator-Demo-Implementation-Plan.md&#96; (in this directory)\n2. **SOP Document:** &#96;../passover-sessions/-25.12.11 - SOP-Verification-System.md&#96;\n3. **Calculator Validation Report:** &#96;../passover-sessions/-25.12.11 - Calculator-Engine-Validation-Report.md&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 PROJECT GOAL\n\nBuild a standalone interactive page where stakeholders can:\n1. Load validated test data (North Battleford)\n2. Input or modify calculator values (62 fields)\n3. See 7 key results in real-time\n4. **See HOW calculations work** (step-by-step breakdown with formulas)\n\n**Key Innovation:** The Walkthrough Panel shows not just WHAT the value is ($1,780,000), but HOW it was calculated with every formula and intermediate step visible.\n\n---\n\n## 🚨 CRITICAL RULES\n\n### Rule 1: DO NOT MODIFY CALCULATOR ENGINE\n- **Location:** &#96;/src/features/report-builder/store/reportBuilderStore.ts&#96;\n- **Function:** &#96;runCalculations()&#96; (lines 5534-5727)\n- **Status:** ✅ VALIDATED - 7/7 exact match with Valcre\n- **Rule:** ❌ **NEVER MODIFY THIS CODE**\n- **Approach:** Import and use as-is, wrap with step-tracking logic\n\n### Rule 2: Must Match Validated Results\nWhen North Battleford test data is loaded, must produce:\n- PGR: $204,240\n- Vacancy Loss: $7,761\n- EGR: $196,479\n- Total Expenses: $84,621\n- NOI: $111,771\n- Raw Value: $1,788,336\n- **Indicated Value: $1,780,000** ✅\n\n### Rule 3: Non-Invasive Wrapper Approach\n- Create &#96;useCalculationSteps()&#96; hook that re-implements calculation logic in parallel\n- Captures intermediate steps for display\n- Does NOT modify validated engine\n- All step-tracking logic is isolated\n- Keeps engine pristine\n\n---\n\n## 🏗️ 3-PANEL ARCHITECTURE\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐",

        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/features/report-builder/README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 77
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Report Builder Feature\n\nA mock V4 report builder implementation demonstrating split-screen editing with live preview.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Navigate to the mock builder\nhttp://localhost:3000/mock-builder\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Features\n\n- **Split-screen layout** with resizable panels\n- **Live preview** with 300ms debouncing on text edits\n- **Drag-and-drop** image reordering\n- **Calculated fields** that auto-update\n- **Tab navigation** between sections\n- **Professional HTML preview** with styling\n\n## Architecture\n\n### State Management\n\nUses Zustand for lightweight, performant state management:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { useReportBuilderStore } from './store/reportBuilderStore';\n\n// In components\nconst sections = useReportBuilderStore((state) =&gt; state.sections);\nconst updateField = useReportBuilderStore((state) =&gt; state.updateFieldValue);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Component Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ncomponents/\n├── ReportBuilderLayout.tsx       # Main split layout\n├── EditPanel/\n│   ├── EditPanel.tsx             # Tab navigation\n│   ├── TextFieldEditor.tsx       # Text inputs (debounced)\n│   ├── ImageFieldEditor.tsx      # Image drag-drop\n│   └── CalculatedFieldDisplay.tsx # Read-only fields\n├── PreviewPanel/\n│   ├── PreviewPanel.tsx          # Preview container\n│   └── PreviewRenderer.tsx       # iframe rendering\n└── sections/\n    ├── CoverInfoSection.tsx      # Cover info fields\n    ├── PhotosSection.tsx         # Photo management\n    └── SummarySection.tsx        # Summary fields\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Data Flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Input → Field Editor → Store Action → State Update → Preview Regeneration → iframe Render\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Mock Sections\n\n### 1. Cover Info\n- Property Address (text, editable)\n- Client Name (text, editable)\n- Report Date (calculated, auto-updated)\n\n### 2. Photos\n- Property Photos (image array, drag-drop reordering)\n- Add/remove images\n- 3 sample Unsplash images\n\n### 3. Summary\n- Summary Notes (multiline text, editable)\n- Total Photo Count (calculated from photos)\n\n## Usage Examples",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "# 🤖 Cursor ↔ Claude Code Automated Testing Workflow\n\n**Created:** September 2, 2025  \n**Status:** ✅ Production Ready (Semi-Automated)  \n**Collaboration:** Cursor + Claude Code + Ben\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 The Vision We Built\n\n**Three-way collaboration:**\n- **Cursor** (fixing bugs) ↔ **Claude Code** (testing with Playwright) ↔ **Cursor** (reading results &amp; auto-fixing)\n- **Ben** provides one-sentence triggers instead of copying entire bug reports\n\n---\n\n## 🏗️ System Architecture\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    AUTOMATION WORKFLOW                        │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n\n  Cursor's World              File System               Claude's World\n  \n┌──────────────┐           ┌─────────────┐           ┌──────────────┐\n│              │           │             │           │              │\n│  Cursor      │  git push │  Git Hook   │  creates  │  Watcher     │\n│  fixes bug   │──────────▶│  post-push  │──────────▶│  detects     │\n│              │           │             │           │  signal      │\n└──────────────┘           └─────────────┘           └──────────────┘\n                                  │                         │\n                                  ▼                         ▼\n                           ┌─────────────┐           ┌──────────────┐\n                           │   Signal    │           │ Test Trigger │",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 3
          },

            "text": "**Status:** ✅ Production Ready (Semi-Automated)  ",

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 10
          },

            "lineNumber": 11
          },

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "# Deployment Stages (New Structure)\n\n## Overview\n\nComponents are organized into deployment stages based on dependencies. Each stage builds on the previous one - foundation components must be stable before adding components that depend on them.\n\n**No timelines or dates** - stages represent dependency order, not delivery schedules.\n\n---\n\n## STAGE 1: Foundation\n\n**Purpose:** Core workflow components that everything else depends on\n\n**Components in this stage:**\n\n### Client Intake Form\n- Embedded from Valta Website\n- Saves to Supabase database\n- Email notification to team\n- **Status:** Complete and stable\n- **Section:** Built-in to main codebase\n\n### Dashboard Interface\n- Job list view with search/filter\n- Job detail view (5 sections)\n- Auto-save on all fields\n- Per-job URL routing\n- **Status:** Complete and stable\n- **Section:** Built-in to main codebase\n\n### Valcre Integration\n- API integration with field mapping\n- Returns VAL number\n- Direct link to Valcre job\n- **Status:** Complete and stable\n- **Section:** Built-in to main codebase\n\n### LOE Generation\n- 4-page HTML LOE document\n- Live preview before sending\n- All job details populated dynamically\n- **Status:** Complete and stable\n- **Section:** Built-in to main codebase\n\n### E-Signature Workflow\n- DocuSeal integration\n- Email via Resend API\n- Webhook to capture signed LOE\n- Updates job status\n- **Status:** 90% complete, testing in progress\n- **Section:** &#96;2-section-ESIGNATURE/&#96;\n\n### ClickUp Task Management\n- Task auto-created on form submission\n- Template with 9 subtasks\n- Task ID stored in database\n- **Status:** Deployed, awaiting acceptance testing\n- **Section:** &#96;3-section-ClickUp-Integration/&#96;\n\n**Dependencies:** None - this is the foundation\n\n---\n\n## STAGE 2: Automation &amp; Workflow Enhancement\n\n**Purpose:** Automated workflows that build on the foundation\n\n**Depends on:** Stage 1 components must be stable\n\n**Components in this stage:**\n\n### N8N Workflow Automations\n- Payment follow-up emails (LOE signed → Stripe link)\n- Payment tracking (Stripe webhook → status updates)",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        },
        "contents": "# 🤖 Claude Code Test Runner Protocol\n\n**Purpose:** Automated Playwright testing triggered by Cursor's git push hooks  \n**Created:** September 2, 2025  \n**For:** Claude Code (me) to execute when Ben says \"run verification tests\"\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start Command\n\nWhen Ben says **\"run verification tests\"** or **\"check for test triggers\"**, execute this workflow:\n\n---\n\n## 📋 Step-by-Step Execution Protocol\n\n### Step 1: Check for Test Trigger\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncat .cursor/test-trigger.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Expected format:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"trigger\": \"cursor-push\",\n  \"commit\": \"abc123...\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"2025-09-02T16:30:00Z\",\n  \"status\": \"testing-needed\",\n  \"tests\": [\n    \"multi-select-property-type\",\n    \"appraisal-fee-save\",\n    \"retainer-amount-save\",\n    \"console-logs-clean\"\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**If file doesn't exist:** Report \"No test trigger found - Cursor hasn't pushed yet\"\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Verify Dev Server Running\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nlsof -i :8080\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**If not running:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm run dev &amp;\nsleep 5  # Wait for server startup\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n### Step 3: Navigate to Dashboard\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nmcp__playwright__browser_navigate\nURL: http://localhost:8080/dashboard\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: Execute Test Suite\n\nRun each test from the trigger file:\n\n#### Test 1: Multi-Select PropertyType\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n1. mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot\n2. Click on a job (e.g., \"Tech Center Building\")\n3. Find PropertyType dropdown\n4. Verify checkbox interface exists\n5. Verify current selections display as tags\n6. Take screenshot: .playwright-mcp/property-type-verification.png\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Pass Criteria:**\n- ✅ Dropdown opens with checkboxes\n- ✅ Multiple selections allowed\n- ✅ Tags display correctly (e.g., \"Building ×\")\n\n---\n\n#### Test 2: Appraisal Fee Save\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n1. Find Appraisal Fee field in snapshot\n2. Click field, change value (e.g., 3500 → 4000)\n3. Click another field to trigger blur/auto-save\n4. Wait 2 seconds (debounce + save time)\n5. Check console messages for errors\n6. Take screenshot if error occurs\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Pass Criteria:**\n- ✅ Field editable\n- ✅ Shows raw value while editing",
        "signatures": {},

        "range": {

        "contents": "# APR Stage 4 Integration - Hybrid PRP Execution Plan\n\n**Created:** January 5, 2026\n**Methodology:** Hybrid PRP (SurferPRP + BMAD + Scout-Plan-Build + Taskmaster)\n**Mission:** Connect Report Builder (Stage 4) to Client Intake Pipeline (Stages 1-3)\n\n---\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis plan applies the Hybrid PRP Methodology to the APR Dashboard's 7-phase integration roadmap. Each phase will be executed using:\n\n- **SCOUT (Haiku Fleet):** Parallel research for API patterns, schema design, component architecture\n- **PLAN (Sonnet):** PRD/PRP generation with BMAD context injection\n- **BUILD (Sonnet + Specialists):** Implementation with domain-specific agents\n- **VALIDATE (QA Gates):** Testing and verification\n\n---\n\n## APR Ecosystem Context\n\n### The Four Stages (Current State)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSTAGE 1: CLIENT INTAKE      --&gt; job_submissions table       [WORKING]\n    |\nSTAGE 2: JOB MANAGEMENT     --&gt; job_loe_details             [WORKING]\n    |\nSTAGE 3: E-SIGNATURE        --&gt; DocuSeal integration        [WORKING]\n    |\nSTAGE 4: REPORT BUILDER     --&gt; 944 fields (DISCONNECTED)   [TARGET]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### The Integration Phases (Roadmap)\n\n| Phase | Name | Description | Status |\n|-------|------|-------------|--------|\n| **0** | Database Setup | Create &#96;report_builder_data&#96; table | Pending |\n| **1** | Expose Home | Add Home section to sidebar | Pending |\n| **2** | Home Fields | Map ~35 fields from Supabase | Pending |\n| **3** | Integration Bridge | Route &#96;/dashboard/job/:jobId/report&#96; | Pending |\n| **4** | Auto-Save | Persistence with 2s debounce | Pending |\n| **5** | Dynamic Lists | Conditions with add/remove | Pending |\n| **6** | Locked Fields | \"Edit in Home\" pattern | Pending |\n| **7** | Page-Editor Sync | Scroll tracking | Pending |\n\n---\n\n## Hybrid Methodology Application\n\n### Phase 0: Database Setup\n\n**Type:** Foundation / Schema\n**Priority:** Critical (blocks all other phases)\n\n#### SCOUT (Haiku Fleet - Parallel)\n\nDeploy 4 Haiku agents simultaneously:\n\n| Agent | Research Focus | Output |\n|-------|---------------|--------|\n| **HK-1** | Current Supabase schema | Tables, relationships, existing JSONB patterns |",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "# APR Dashboard v3 - Agent Documentation\n\n&gt; **Purpose:** Quick-start context for agents working on this codebase\n&gt; **Read time:** 3-5 minutes for full orientation\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n**New to this project?** Read in order:\n1. &#96;system/4-stage-pipeline.md&#96; - Understand the full system\n2. &#96;system/field-registry-overview.md&#96; - How the 1,687 fields work\n3. &#96;system/zustand-store-patterns.md&#96; - State management approach\n\n**Working on a task?** Check:\n- &#96;tasks/&#96; - Any existing PRDs or implementation plans\n- &#96;sops/&#96; - Standard procedures for common operations\n\n---\n\n## When to Read What\n\n| If you need to... | Read this |\n|-------------------|-----------|\n| Understand the overall system | &#96;system/4-stage-pipeline.md&#96; |\n| Add or modify fields | &#96;system/field-registry-overview.md&#96; + &#96;sops/adding-report-fields.md&#96; |\n| Work with state/store | &#96;system/zustand-store-patterns.md&#96; |\n| Test your changes | &#96;sops/testing-changes.md&#96; |\n| Understand current tasks | &#96;tasks/&#96; folder |\n\n---\n\n## Project At a Glance\n\n**What:** APR Dashboard v3 is a 4-stage appraisal workflow system\n\n**Stages:**\n1. Client Intake - Job submission form\n2. Job Management - LOE preparation\n3. E-Signature - DocuSeal integration\n4. Report Builder - Final report assembly (944+ fields)\n\n**Tech Stack:**\n- React 18 + TypeScript\n- Zustand for state management\n- Supabase for backend\n- Tailwind CSS\n\n**Key Locations:**\n| Purpose | Path |\n|---------|------|\n| Field definitions | &#96;src/features/report-builder/schema/fieldRegistry.ts&#96; |\n| Zustand store | &#96;src/features/report-builder/store/reportBuilderStore.ts&#96; |\n| HTML template | &#96;public/Report-MF-template.html&#96; |\n| Test data | &#96;src/features/report-builder/data/TestDataSet1.ts&#96; |\n| Knowledge hub | &#96;docs/bc research &amp; notes/TDD-PAGE-ARCHITECTURE.md&#96; |\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Map\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n.agent/\n├── README.md              &lt;- You are here (index)\n├── tasks/                 &lt;- PRDs and implementation plans\n│   └── .gitkeep\n├── system/                &lt;- Architecture documentation\n│   ├── 4-stage-pipeline.md\n│   ├── field-registry-overview.md",
        "signatures": {},

          },
          {
            "text": "## Project At a Glance",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "**What:** APR Dashboard v3 is a 4-stage appraisal workflow system",
            "lineNumber": 35

            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "**Stages:**",

            "text": "2. Job Management - LOE preparation",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "3. E-Signature - DocuSeal integration",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Report Builder - Final report assembly (944+ fields)",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },

          },

      "codeBlock": {

        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},

        "contents": "# Session Continuity System - Master Guide\n\n**Last Updated:** November 7, 2025\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start for Agents\n\nWhen you start a session, you have THREE knowledge sources:\n\n1. **Passover files** → Treasure maps pointing to what matters\n2. **Memory system** → Search ALL sessions with &#96;/check-all-memory [keywords]&#96; (PRIMARY - use 90% of time)\n3. **JSONL conversation logs** → Raw session transcripts\n\n**Don't just read files. Search memory FIRST. You're not a file reader - you're a knowledge retriever.**\n\n**Search Strategy (from startup protocol):**\n- 🏆 **PRIMARY (90%):** &#96;/check-all-memory [keywords]&#96; - Searches ALL sessions, always current\n- 🔄 **FALLBACK (10%):** &#96;mcp__cognee__search \"query\" GRAPH_COMPLETION&#96; - For vague/conceptual queries\n- 📋 **OTHER:** &#96;pmem \"query\"&#96; - Legacy shorthand (use /check-all-memory instead)\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle\n\n**Passover files are TREASURE MAPS, not summaries.**\n\nThey tell you:\n- What files to read\n- What memory searches to run (and WHY)\n- What JSONL sections matter\n- What's broken or blocked\n- Critical context from previous work\n\n**The passover file guides you. Memory system empowers you. JSONL gives you raw detail.**\n\n---\n\n## File Types in This System\n\n### 0. Master Evergreen Overview (READ THIS FIRST)\n\n**Format:** &#96;00-SYSTEM-OVERVIEW-EVERGREEN.md&#96;\n\n**Created by:** Manual/Agent, maintained across sessions\n\n**Purpose:** Master system overview - complete project context\n\n**Contains:**\n- Complete system architecture\n- All integrations and field mappings\n- Open issues and bugs\n- Production status\n- Client context\n- Search keywords\n\n**Example:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n00-SYSTEM-OVERVIEW-EVERGREEN.md\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Your job:** READ THIS FILE FIRST before any session work. This is your foundation context.\n\n**Naming:** &#96;00-&#96; prefix ensures it appears first alphabetically for easy discovery.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Session Work Files (CURRENT STANDARD - Nov 2025)\n\n**Format:** &#96;YY.MM.DD-[N] - [Descriptive-Name].md&#96;\n\n**Date Format:** Use DOTS: &#96;25.11.19&#96; ✅ (NOT dashes: ~~25-11-19~~)",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/03-ClickUp-Integration/README.md",
        "range": {

        "contents": "# ClickUp Integration Rebuild - Complete Implementation Package\n\n**Package Date**: November 4, 2025 (Updated: November 19, 2025)\n**Status**: 4-Stage System in Development\n**Document Set**: 01-06 (6 documents, ~125KB total)\n\n---\n\n## What This Package Is\n\nThis is a **complete rebuild specification** for the APR Dashboard ClickUp integration. The current implementation works backwards from what's needed - this package provides everything required to rebuild it correctly.\n\n**Current (Wrong) Flow:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nForm Submitted → Dashboard → Manual Button Click → ClickUp Task\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**New (Correct) Flow:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nForm Submitted → Auto-Create ClickUp Task → Team Notification → Dashboard Work\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Package Updates\n\n### November 19, 2025 - 🔧 4-STAGE AUTOMATION SYSTEM\n\n**Status:** In Development - Stages 1 &amp; 2 Deployed, Issues Found\n\n**Evolution:** Expanded from 2-stage to **4-stage progressive automation system**\n\n**4-Stage Flow:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSTAGE 1: Form Submission → Create ClickUp Task\n   ↓\nSTAGE 2: Valcre Job Created → Add LOE Section\n   ↓\nSTAGE 2.5: DocuSeal LOE Sent → Add Sent Timestamp\n   ↓\nSTAGE 3: DocuSeal LOE Signed → Add Signed Timestamp + Name\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Current Configuration:**\n- API Token: &#96;pk_10791838_XB273RX0O9O1AL5WTZZIVREX1F3RUOL5&#96; (Nov 19 validated via JSONL search)\n- List ID: &#96;901706896375&#96; (New Submission - BC Workspace)\n- Workspace ID: &#96;8555561&#96; (BC Workspace - Development)\n- Test Script: &#96;/test-clickup-4-stages.sh&#96;\n\n**Test Results:**\n- ✅ **Stage 1**: Creates task successfully with proper format\n- ⚠️ **Stage 2**: Working BUT creates new task instead of updating Stage 1 (CRITICAL BUG)\n- ❌ **Stage 2.5**: \"Job not found, skipping ClickUp update\" - webhook lookup failing\n- ❌ **Stage 3**: \"Job not found for submission\" - same webhook issue\n\n**Critical Issues Found:**\n1. **Stage 2 creates new task** - Should update Stage 1 task, not create different task ID\n2. **DocuSeal webhook can't find jobs** - Stages 2.5 &amp; 3 failing job lookup\n3. **Field name mismatches** - Agent 2 peer review found wrong field names in code\n\n**New Documentation:**\n- &#96;05-4-STAGE-AUTOMATION-REFERENCE.md&#96; - Complete 4-stage system documentation",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# ClickUp Integration Rebuild - Complete Implementation Package",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/15-Contract-review/1-Formatting-template/PLAN-Frontend-Template-Expert-Agent.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 67
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Plan: APR Frontend &amp; Template Expert Agent\n\n**Created:** December 27, 2025\n**Project:** APR Dashboard v3 - Report-MF-Template Management\n**Goal:** Create a specialized Claude Code agent that combines frontend development + template management expertise\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nBen currently uses Claude Desktop for editing Report-MF-template pages because:\n- Easy HTML preview\n- Page-by-page focused editing\n- Good for styling work\n\nBut Claude Desktop can't:\n- Read local codebase files\n- Commit to git\n- Manage version control\n- Validate against fieldRegistry\n- Know the 4-file sync system\n\n**Solution:** Create a hybrid agent in Claude Code that does BOTH.\n\n---\n\n## Agent: &#96;apr-frontend-template-expert&#96;\n\n### Core Capabilities\n\n**1. Frontend Development (like Desktop agent)**\n- Edit HTML pages with proper styling\n- Understand the 77-page report structure\n- Work with CSS scoped to &#96;.page-XX&#96; classes\n- Know the placeholder pattern &#96;{{field-id}}&#96;\n- Preview workflow: knows localhost:8082/mock-builder\n\n**2. Template Management (codebase integration)**\n- Maintain &#96;/public/Report-MF-template.html&#96; (canonical)\n- Version control with changelog in HTML comment\n- Validate field IDs against &#96;fieldRegistry.ts&#96;\n- 4-file sync awareness (registry, test data, template, images)\n- Git commits with proper messages\n\n**3. Desktop Handoff**\n- Export pages/template for Desktop continuation\n- Import edits from Desktop and merge\n- Maintain working copies in accessible location\n- Track which pages were edited and by whom\n\n---\n\n## File Structure\n\n### Agent Definition\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n~/.claude/agents/00-Custom-Agents/apr-frontend-template-expert.md\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Agent Workspace (THIS FOLDER)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v3/docs/15-Contract-review/1-Formatting-template/\n├── README.md                               # Agent instructions (update)\n├── TEMPLATE-STATUS.md                      # Current version, changelog (CREATE)\n├── MASTER-PAGE-FIELD-REFERENCE.md          # 93KB page field reference (EXISTS)\n│\n├── -Template-Master Doc Previewer/         # Individual pages &amp; field mappings",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Plan: APR Frontend &amp; Template Expert Agent",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2

          },
          {

            "text": "---",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8

            "text": "## Problem Statement",

            "text": "Ben currently uses Claude Desktop for editing Report-MF-template pages because:",
            "lineNumber": 11

          },
          {

          {

        "contents": "# APR Dashboard v3 - Complete Architecture Overview\n\n## Table of Contents\n1. [System Overview](#system-overview)\n2. [Stage 1: Client Intake &amp; Job Creation](#stage-1-client-intake--job-creation)\n3. [Stage 2: Job Management &amp; LOE Preparation](#stage-2-job-management--loe-preparation)\n4. [Stage 3: E-Signature Flow](#stage-3-e-signature-flow)\n5. [Stage 4: Report Builder (Phase 2)](#stage-4-report-builder-phase-2)\n6. [Data Architecture](#data-architecture)\n7. [Integration Points](#integration-points)\n8. [File Structure Reference](#file-structure-reference)\n\n---\n\n## System Overview\n\nAPR Dashboard v3 is a comprehensive appraisal management system that orchestrates the entire workflow from client submission to final report generation. The system is built on **React + TypeScript** with **Supabase** as the backend database and integrates with multiple third-party services.\n\n### Core Technology Stack\n- **Frontend**: React 18, TypeScript, Vite\n- **UI Framework**: shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS\n- **State Management**: Zustand (for report builder), React Context (for job list)\n- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage + Edge Functions)\n- **Routing**: React Router v6\n- **External Integrations**: Valcre API, DocuSeal API, ClickUp API, Google Drive API\n\n### Application Flow\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nClient Form → Supabase → Dashboard → Valcre → LOE Generation → DocuSeal → \nClient Signs → Status Update → Report Builder → Final Report\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1: Client Intake &amp; Job Creation\n\n### Overview\nClients submit appraisal requests through a public-facing form. The form captures client information, property details, and optional file uploads. All data is immediately saved to Supabase.\n\n### Key Components\n\n#### 1. Client Intake Form (&#96;/&#96; route)\n**File**: &#96;src/pages/Index.tsx&#96;\n- Public-facing form (no authentication required)\n- Multi-section form with validation\n- File upload capability\n- Auto-fill from client profile (if exists)\n\n**Form Sections**:\n- Client Information (name, email, phone, organization, address)\n- Property Information (name, address, type, intended use, valuation premises)",

        "contents": "# APR Dashboard - Project Index &amp; Workflow Guide\n\n**Last Updated:** November 7, 2025\n**Project Status:** Active Development\n**Current Focus:** ClickUp Integration • Valcre Sync Issues\n\n---\n\n## 📖 HOW TO USE THIS PROJECT (READ THIS EVERY SESSION!)\n\n**Dear Agent (that's you!),**\n\nWhen you start a new session, here's your workflow:\n\n1. **✅ YOU JUST READ THIS FILE** - Good! This is the project-level README\n2. **📂 CHECK THE SECTION INDEX BELOW** - See what we worked on last\n3. **📄 NAVIGATE TO THAT SECTION'S README** - Get full current state and context\n4. **🚀 START WORKING** - You now have complete context!\n\n**Example:**\n- This README says: \"Last worked on: 03-ClickUp-Integration\"\n- You navigate to: &#96;.docs/03-ClickUp-Integration/README.md&#96;\n- You read the current state, config, known issues, next steps\n- You're ready to continue where we left off!\n\n---\n\n## 📋 DOCUMENTATION STRATEGY (IMPORTANT!)\n\n**README.md = Source of Truth**\n- Each section has a README.md that's the ONLY file you need to read\n- README shows: Current State, Configuration, Known Issues, Next Steps\n- Always update the README when work is done\n- This is what Cursor and other agents naturally read first\n\n**Session Continuity:**\n- Session handoff notes exist in &#96;.docs/-passover | work-sessions/&#96; folder\n- Use the &#96;/session-summary&#96; command for proper session documentation\n- README files contain current state, not session history\n\n**Why this works:**\n- One file to read = fast onboarding\n- Always up-to-date (we update it as we work)\n- No hunting through old sessions\n- Clear handoff between agents\n\n---\n\n## 📊 PROJECT STATE OVERVIEW\n\n### What's Working ✅\n- **Form Submissions:** Both APR Dashboard + Valta Website forms working\n- **ClickUp Auto-Creation:** Form submission → ClickUp task (LIVE!)\n- **Database:** Supabase, migrations applied, triggers working\n- **Deployments:** Vercel CLI for fast deploys (~5 seconds)\n\n### In Progress 🔄\n- **Valcre Sync UX:** Needs improvement (annoying popups, sync before job exists)\n- **Field Mapping:** Some fields not mapping correctly to Valcre",

        "contents": "# ClickUp Integration Reference\n\n**Status:** 🔧 In Development - Stages 1 &amp; 2 Deployed, Issues Found\n**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025\n**Architecture:** Progressive 4-stage task updates through complete appraisal lifecycle\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Overview](#overview)\n2. [Stage Progression](#stage-progression)\n3. [Stage 1: Form Submission → Create Task](#stage-1-form-submission--create-clickup-task)\n4. [Stage 2: Valcre Job Created → Add LOE Section](#stage-2-valcre-job-created--add-loe-section)\n5. [Stage 2.5: DocuSeal Send → Add LOE Sent Timestamp](#stage-25-docuseal-send--add-loe-sent-timestamp)\n6. [Stage 3: DocuSeal Signature → Add LOE Signed](#stage-3-docuseal-signature--add-loe-signed)\n7. [Task Name Formats](#task-name-formats)\n8. [Configuration](#configuration)\n9. [Test Suite](#test-suite)\n10. [Known Issues](#known-issues)\n11. [Next Steps](#next-steps)\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nAutomated ClickUp task management system that tracks the complete appraisal job lifecycle through 4 distinct stages, from initial form submission to signed LOE.\n\n**Architecture:** Progressive task updates - each stage adds information while preserving previous stage data.\n\n### Key Characteristics\n\n- **4-Stage Progression**: Form → Valcre → DocuSeal Send → DocuSeal Signed\n- **Progressive Updates**: Each stage appends to previous stages (no data loss)\n- **Single Task ID**: Same task updated through all stages (not separate tasks)\n- **Markdown Descriptions**: All data stored in formatted task descriptions\n- **Event-Driven**: Database triggers and webhooks drive automation\n- **VAL Naming**: Uses new VAL job numbers (not old CAL method)\n\n---\n\n## Stage Progression\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSTAGE 1: Form Submission\n   ↓ (creates task)\nSTAGE 2: Valcre Job Created\n   ↓ (adds LOE section)\nSTAGE 2.5: DocuSeal LOE Sent\n   ↓ (adds timestamp)\nSTAGE 3: DocuSeal LOE Signed\n   ↓ (adds signature + name)\nCOMPLETE: Full task history\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1: Form Submission → Create ClickUp Task\n\n**Trigger:** Client submits appraisal request form on valta.ca",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "## Overview",

          },
          {
            "text": "Automated ClickUp task management system that tracks the complete appraisal job lifecycle through 4 distinct stages, from initial form submission to signed LOE.",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "**Architecture:** Progressive task updates - each stage adds information while preserving previous stage data.",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "### Key Characteristics",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "- **4-Stage Progression**: Form → Valcre → DocuSeal Send → DocuSeal Signed",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Progressive Updates**: Each stage appends to previous stages (no data loss)",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Single Task ID**: Same task updated through all stages (not separate tasks)",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Markdown Descriptions**: All data stored in formatted task descriptions",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Event-Driven**: Database triggers and webhooks drive automation",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "- **VAL Naming**: Uses new VAL job numbers (not old CAL method)",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "## Stage Progression",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "STAGE 1: Form Submission",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "   ↓ (creates task)",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "STAGE 2: Valcre Job Created",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "   ↓ (adds LOE section)",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "STAGE 2.5: DocuSeal LOE Sent",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "   ↓ (adds timestamp)",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "STAGE 3: DocuSeal LOE Signed",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "   ↓ (adds signature + name)",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "COMPLETE: Full task history",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "## Stage 1: Form Submission → Create ClickUp Task",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "**Trigger:** Client submits appraisal request form on valta.ca",
            "lineNumber": 60
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.25512969493865967
    },

        "contents": "# Implementation Plan: Interactive Calculator Demonstration Page\n\n**Project:** APR Dashboard V3\n**Date:** 2025-12-12\n**Purpose:** Build standalone interactive calculator demo showcasing validated income capitalization engine\n**Status:** 📋 PLANNING COMPLETE - Ready for Implementation\n\n---\n\n## 🔍 SEARCH KEYWORDS\n\n&#96;calculator-demo-page&#96; &#96;interactive-calculator&#96; &#96;calculation-walkthrough&#96; &#96;validated-calculator-engine&#96; &#96;three-panel-layout&#96; &#96;input-output-breakdown&#96; &#96;calculation-transparency&#96; &#96;step-by-step-formulas&#96; &#96;north-battleford-test-data&#96; &#96;7-metric-validation&#96; &#96;non-invasive-wrapper&#96; &#96;useCalculationSteps-hook&#96; &#96;income-capitalization-demo&#96; &#96;stakeholder-transparency&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis plan outlines the creation of a standalone interactive calculator demonstration page that showcases the validated income capitalization calculator engine. The page will allow stakeholders to input values, load test data, and see a step-by-step calculation breakdown showing HOW results are derived - providing full transparency into the calculation methodology.\n\n**Key Innovation:** The Walkthrough Panel shows not just WHAT the value is ($1,780,000), but HOW it was calculated with every formula and intermediate step visible.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Architecture Overview\n\n### Current State Analysis\n\n**Validated Calculator Engine:**\n- **Location:** &#96;/src/features/report-builder/store/reportBuilderStore.ts&#96;\n- **Function:** &#96;runCalculations()&#96; (lines 5534-5727)\n- **Status:** ✅ **VALIDATED** - 7/7 exact match with Valcre appraisal workbook\n- **Rule:** ❌ **DO NOT MODIFY** - Import and use as-is\n\n**Test Data:**\n- **Location:** &#96;/src/features/report-builder/data/northBattlefordTestData-REAL.ts&#96;\n- **Contains:** 62+ calculator fields with validated North Battleford data\n- **Expected Output:** $1,780,000 indicated value (validated)\n\n**Existing Components:**\n- TestInputDashboard: Field input interface at &#96;/src/features/test-input/TestInputDashboard.tsx&#96;\n- UI Components: Full shadcn/ui component library available\n- Store: Zustand store with calculation engine already implemented",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/15-Contract-review/0-APR-Core-to org/Field &amp; Template Testing.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 81
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Field &amp; Template Testing Guide\n\n**Created:** 2026-01-04\n**Updated:** 2026-01-04\n**Purpose:** Document field creation, testing methods, and debugging workflow\n\n---\n\n## Key Definitions\n\n### fieldRegistry.ts\n\n**Single source of truth** that defines every field in the system.\n\n| Property | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| &#96;id&#96; | Unique field identifier |\n| &#96;label&#96; | Display name |\n| &#96;type&#96; | text, number, date, dropdown, etc. |\n| &#96;section&#96; | Which section it belongs to (cover, site, calc, etc.) |\n| &#96;inputSource&#96; | 'user-input' or 'calculated' |\n| &#96;defaultValue&#96; | Starting value (optional) |\n\n**What it does:**\n- Tells the Store (App Field Values) what fields exist and how to build them\n- Tells the calc engine which fields are inputs vs outputs\n- Reference for TestDataSet1 to know what IDs are valid\n\n**File:** &#96;/src/features/report-builder/schema/fieldRegistry.ts&#96;\n\n---\n\n### Terminology: App Fields vs Scripts\n\n**App Fields** = The fields in Editor Panel where values live\n**Scripts** = Functions that perform actions (load, save, calculate)\n\n**The file &#96;reportBuilderStore.ts&#96; contains BOTH:**\n- App Fields data structure\n- Scripts that manipulate them\n\n**DO NOT use the word \"store\"** - it's confusing. Say:\n- \"Load data into App Fields\" ✓\n- \"Script reads from TestDataSet1\" ✓\n- ~~\"Load into store\"~~ ✗\n\n---\n\n### Data vs Scripts\n\n**DATA:** &#96;TestDataSet1.ts&#96; - Contains all test field values for sample property\n\n**SCRIPTS:** Functions that perform ACTIONS on the data\n- &#96;loadDataSet1All&#96; - Test 2 action (loads ALL fields to TDD page)\n- &#96;loadDataSet1User&#96; - Test 3 action (loads DataSet1 user inputs → calc)\n\n---\n\n## Step 0 - Field Creation\n\nBefore testing, fields must be created in three places:\n\n### Step 0a - Define Field (fieldRegistry.ts)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n{\n  id: 'city',\n  storeId: 'city',\n  label: 'City',\n  type: 'text',\n  section: 'cover',\n  inputSource: 'user-input'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 0b - Add to EditPanel Layout (EditPanel.tsx)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nconst COVER_FIELD_LAYOUT = [\n  { id: 'city', label: 'City' },\n  // ...\n];\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/APR-Domain Mgr/FULL-ECOSYSTEM-INTEGRATION.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

        "contents": "- [ ] Route: &#96;/dashboard/job/:jobId/report&#96; works\n\n---\n\n## Platform Independence Strategy\n\n&gt; **Objective:** Eliminate Valcre, ClickUp, and Pipedrive dependencies. Build a complete appraisal business platform on owned infrastructure.\n\n### Strategic Direction\n\nAPR Dashboard v3 is evolving from a \"report builder with integrations\" to a **complete appraisal business platform**. The goal is platform independence - owning the full stack rather than stitching together SaaS tools.\n\n### Target State\n\n| Function | Current | Target | Status |\n|----------|---------|--------|--------|\n| Report Generation | Valcre | Report Builder | ~80% complete |\n| Project Management | ClickUp | Job Mgt v3 | ~60% complete |\n| CRM / Pipeline | Pipedrive | Acquisition Pipeline | ~40% complete |\n| E-Signatures | DocuSeal | DocuSeal (keep) | Complete |\n| Database | Supabase | Supabase (keep) | Complete |\n| File Storage | Google Drive | Supabase Storage | Working |\n\n### Tech Stack Simplification\n\n**Before:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSupabase + Valcre + ClickUp + Pipedrive + DocuSeal + Google Drive\n    |         |         |          |          |           |\n  Core    Reports    Tasks       CRM      E-Sign      Files\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**After:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSupabase + DocuSeal\n    |          |\nEverything   E-Sign\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Three Workstreams\n\n#### Workstream A: Report Builder (Kill Valcre)\n**Goal:** Complete Valcre replacement with specialized section editors\n\n**Done:**\n- Field registry (944 fields)\n- Calculator engine (277 metrics, validated)\n- HTML template system (70+ pages)\n- Live preview with PostMessage bridge (500+ fields)\n- Income/Sales/Cost approach panels\n\n**Remaining:**\n- Home Tab (workbook setup, common fields)\n- Image Manager (grid, reorder, layout)\n- Narrative Editor (rich text, templates)\n- Data persistence (auto-save to Supabase)\n- Job integration (pre-populate from job_submissions)\n\n#### Workstream B: Job Management (Kill ClickUp)\n**Goal:** Replace ClickUp with built-in job/task management\n\n**Done:**\n- Job list view with search/filter\n- Job detail view with accordion sections",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- [ ] Route: &#96;/dashboard/job/:jobId/report&#96; works",
            "lineNumber": 626
          },

            "lineNumber": 628

            "lineNumber": 636

          {
            "lineNumber": 650

            "lineNumber": 652

        "contents": "# APR Dashboard - Test Scenarios Tracking\n\n**Date Created:** December 23, 2025\n**Purpose:** Keep organized between two distinct test scenarios\n**Status:** In Progress\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 The Two Test Scenarios (CRITICAL - DON'T MIX THESE UP!)\n\n### Test 1: Full Template Mapping Test\n**Purpose:** Verify template completeness\n**File:** &#96;northBattlefordTestData.ts&#96; (production) or &#96;TEST-DATA-COMPLETE-V2.json&#96; (reference)\n**Field Count:** 1,778 values (covers ~1,000+ template placeholders)\n**What It Tests:** THE TEMPLATE\n\n**Details:**\n- Every field placeholder in template receives data\n- Example: \"Does &#96;comp1-saleprice&#96; show up on page 47 in the right cell?\"\n- NO calculations involved - just field injection\n- Visual confirmation that template renders complete 71-page report\n- Tests field ID → template location mapping\n\n**Success Criteria:**\n✅ All template placeholders populate\n✅ Template renders complete 71-page report\n✅ No {{missing-placeholders}} in output\n\n**Status:** ✅ **READY FOR PREVIEW TEST** (per EXTRACTION-STATUS-HANDOFF.md)\n**Assigned To:** Ready for testing with \"Preview in Builder\"\n\n---\n\n### Test 2: Human Input → Calculation → Output Test\n**Purpose:** Verify the actual user workflow\n**File:** &#96;HUMAN-INPUT-TEST-DATA.json&#96; (✅ CREATED - 774 fields)\n**Field Count:** 774 input fields (user-input only)\n**What It Tests:** THE SYSTEM\n\n**Details:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nDASHBOARD (~575 input fields only)\n    ↓\nAppraiser enters data (or clicks \"Load Test Data\")\n    ↓\n[CALC ENGINE RUNS]\n    - Sums expenses\n    - Computes NOI\n    - Applies comp adjustments\n    - Derives per-unit metrics\n    ↓\n~200 calculated fields generated\n    ↓\n575 inputs + 200 calculated = ~775 total fields\n    ↓\nTEMPLATE renders report\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**What Gets Excluded from Input File:**\n- ❌ Calculated outputs (NOI, totals, per-unit metrics)\n- ❌ Duplicates\n- ❌ System-generated (photourl, geocode, id)\n\n**Success Criteria:**\n✅ Dashboard shows ONLY ~575 input fields (no calculated)\n✅ Calc engine generates ~200 calculated fields\n✅ 775 total fields (575 + 200) flow to template correctly",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/APR-Domain Mgr/DOMAIN-CONTEXT.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 69
          }
        },
        "contents": "# APR Dashboard v3 - Orchestrator Context\n\n&gt; **Last Updated:** 2026-01-05\n&gt; **Mission Status:** Active\n\n---\n\n## Mission\n\nBuild a complete 4-stage appraisal workflow where data flows from client intake through to final PDF report - no re-entry, no data loss, full persistence.\n\n---\n\n## The Four Stages\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSTAGE 1: CLIENT INTAKE      --&gt; job_submissions table\n    |\nSTAGE 2: JOB MANAGEMENT     --&gt; job_loe_details, job_property_info\n    |\nSTAGE 3: E-SIGNATURE        --&gt; DocuSeal integration\n    |\nSTAGE 4: REPORT BUILDER     --&gt; Complete appraisal report (944 fields)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Current State:** Stages 1-3 work. Stage 4 (Report Builder) is disconnected - no persistence, no bridge to job data.\n\n---\n\n## Phases (Integration Roadmap)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[ ] Phase 0: Database setup (report_builder_data table)\n[ ] Phase 1: Expose Home section in sidebar\n[ ] Phase 2: Add ~35 Home section fields (mapped from Supabase)\n[ ] Phase 3: Integration bridge route (/dashboard/job/:jobId/report)\n[ ] Phase 4: Auto-save + persistence (2s debounce)\n[ ] Phase 5: Dynamic lists for conditions\n[ ] Phase 6: Locked fields + \"Edit in Home\" pattern\n[ ] Phase 7: Page-to-editor sync (scroll tracking)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Current Focus\n\n**Active Phase:** Pre-Phase 0 (foundation work, standalone components)\n\n**Current Objective:** Build standalone components that will plug into Report Builder\n\n**Blockers:** None - deciding what to tackle next\n\n---\n\n## Key Decisions Made\n\n1. **Standalone-first development:** Build features like image configurator in isolation at &#96;/image-test&#96;, then wire into Report Builder. Rationale: Easier to perfect without Report Builder complexity.\n\n2. **Home section for job data:** All data from Stages 1-3 lands in a new \"Home\" section in Report Builder. Other sections reference Home fields. Rationale: Single source of truth, locked fields prevent drift.\n\n3. **JSONB storage for report data:** Store entire report state as JSONB in &#96;report_builder_data&#96; table. Rationale: Flexible schema, easy versioning, matches Zustand store shape.\n\n---\n\n## Micro to Macro Map\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMICRO: Image configurator component\n  |",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/15-Contract-review/3-Calc Eng &amp; Plan Agent/-Planning-Notes-Calculator-Demo.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 95
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 176
          }
        },
        "contents": "└── types/\n    └── calculatorDemo.types.ts\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🔑 KEY COMPONENTS\n\n### InputPanel.tsx\n- 62 calculator fields organized in collapsible sections\n- Categories: Unit Mix, Other Income, Vacancy, Expenses, Cap Rate, Adjustments\n- Visual distinction: editable (white) vs calculated (gray)\n- Real-time validation\n- Connected to Zustand store\n\n### OutputPanel.tsx\n- 7 key validated results in Card components\n- Color coding: Income (green), Expenses (red), Value (blue)\n- Large prominent display for final $1,780,000 value\n- Tooltips explaining each metric\n- Updates in real-time as inputs change\n\n### WalkthroughPanel.tsx (THE INNOVATION)\nShows step-by-step breakdown with formulas:\n\n**Example Display:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nStep 1: Unit Mix\n  Type 1 (1BR/1BA): 16 units × $1,064/mo × 12 = $204,288\n  Total Units: 16\n  Total Rental Revenue: $204,288\n\nStep 2: Other Income\n  Parking: $375/unit/mo × 16 × 12 = $6,000\n  Laundry: $150/unit/mo × 16 × 12 = $2,400\n  Total Other Income: $8,400\n\nStep 3: Potential Gross Revenue\n  PGR = Rental Revenue + Other Income\n  PGR = $204,288 + $8,400 = $212,688\n\n[...continues through all 8 steps...]\n\nStep 8: Final Indicated Value\n  Raw Value: $1,913,952\n  Rounded: $1,910,000\n  Adjustments: $0\n  Final Value: $1,910,000\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 🔧 TECHNICAL APPROACH\n\n### Data Flow\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Input (62 fields)\n    ↓\nZustand Store (reportBuilderStore)\n    ↓\nrunCalculations() [VALIDATED ENGINE - DON'T TOUCH]\n    ↓\nuseCalculationSteps() [NEW HOOK - Parallel tracking]\n    ↓\n3 Outputs:\n    ├─ Input Panel (shows field values)\n    ├─ Output Panel (shows 7 results)\n    └─ Walkthrough Panel (shows HOW)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### useCalculationSteps Hook Strategy\n\n**Problem:** Calculator engine doesn't expose intermediate values\n\n**Solution:** Non-invasive wrapper\n1. Subscribe to all relevant store fields\n2. Re-implement same calculation logic in parallel\n3. Capture each step with formula, inputs, result\n4. Return formatted CalculationStep objects\n5. Display in WalkthroughPanel",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "    └── calculatorDemo.types.ts",

          },
          {
            "text": "- 62 calculator fields organized in collapsible sections",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {

          {

          },
          {
            "text": "### OutputPanel.tsx",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "- 7 key validated results in Card components",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "- Color coding: Income (green), Expenses (red), Value (blue)",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "- Large prominent display for final $1,780,000 value",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "- Tooltips explaining each metric",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "- Updates in real-time as inputs change",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "### WalkthroughPanel.tsx (THE INNOVATION)",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "Shows step-by-step breakdown with formulas:",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "**Example Display:**",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "Step 1: Unit Mix",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "  Type 1 (1BR/1BA): 16 units × $1,064/mo × 12 = $204,288",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "  Total Units: 16",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "  Total Rental Revenue: $204,288",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "Step 2: Other Income",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "  Parking: $375/unit/mo × 16 × 12 = $6,000",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "  Laundry: $150/unit/mo × 16 × 12 = $2,400",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "  Total Other Income: $8,400",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": "Step 3: Potential Gross Revenue",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "  PGR = Rental Revenue + Other Income",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "  PGR = $204,288 + $8,400 = $212,688",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "[...continues through all 8 steps...]",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "Step 8: Final Indicated Value",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "text": "  Raw Value: $1,913,952",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "  Rounded: $1,910,000",
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "text": "  Adjustments: $0",

          },
          {
            "text": "  Final Value: $1,910,000",

          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "text": "## 🔧 TECHNICAL APPROACH",
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "text": "### Data Flow",
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "text": "User Input (62 fields)",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "text": "    ↓",
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "text": "Zustand Store (reportBuilderStore)",
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "text": "    ↓",
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "runCalculations() [VALIDATED ENGINE - DON'T TOUCH]",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "    ↓",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "useCalculationSteps() [NEW HOOK - Parallel tracking]",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "    ↓",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "3 Outputs:",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "    ├─ Input Panel (shows field values)",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "    ├─ Output Panel (shows 7 results)",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "    └─ Walkthrough Panel (shows HOW)",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "### useCalculationSteps Hook Strategy",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "**Problem:** Calculator engine doesn't expose intermediate values",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "text": "**Solution:** Non-invasive wrapper",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Subscribe to all relevant store fields",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Re-implement same calculation logic in parallel",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Capture each step with formula, inputs, result",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Return formatted CalculationStep objects",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "5. Display in WalkthroughPanel",
            "lineNumber": 175
          }
        ]
      },

        "contents": "# Registry &amp; Workbook Expert\n\n**Purpose:** Activate expertise for field registry management and Valcre workbook logic.\n\n---\n\n## Role\n\nYou are the **Registry &amp; Workbook Expert** - the specialist who understands:\n- How field IDs in our system map to Valcre workbook named ranges\n- The origin of all calculation logic (Direct Cap, Sales Comp, Cost Approach)\n- Field verification protocols before adding/modifying registry entries\n\n---\n\n## Persona Switching (IMPORTANT)\n\n**When NOT deploying subagents, switch to the appropriate persona yourself:**\n\n| Work Type | Switch To | Why |\n|-----------|-----------|-----|\n| Registry/Schema work | **typescript-pro** | Type-safe field definitions, schema patterns |\n| React components (EditPanel, PreviewPanel) | **react-specialist** | React 18+ hooks, performance, component patterns |\n| General frontend/UI | **frontend-developer** | UI engineering, web standards, accessibility |\n| Testing/Verification | **qa-expert** | Systematic testing, quality metrics |\n| Store/State management | **typescript-pro** | Zustand patterns, type safety |\n| Build/Deploy issues | **devops-engineer** | CI/CD, build configuration |\n\n**How to switch:** State your persona at the start of focused work:\n&gt; \"Switching to react-specialist for this EditPanel work...\"\n\n**Default for this domain:** **TypeScript-Pro** for registry and store work.\n\n---\n\n## Core Reference Files\n\n### 1. APR Domain Core Management Folder (YOUR WORKSPACE)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v3/docs/15-Contract-review/0-APR-Domain-Core-Mgt/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n**This is your dedicated workspace.** Contains:\n- &#96;APR-DOMAIN-KNOWLEDGE-25.12.26.md&#96; - **PRIMARY REFERENCE** - Field counts, calc engine status, valuation approaches\n- &#96;CALC-ENGINE-FIELD-MAP.md&#96; - Maps calc engine to field registry\n- &#96;VALCRE-TO-TEMPLATE-CROSSWALK.md&#96; - Valcre → Template field mapping\n- &#96;VALCRE_REPORT_STRUCTURE.md&#96; - 88-sheet workbook structure\n- &#96;VALCRE_TABLE_FIELD_MAPPING.md&#96; - Table-level field mappings\n- &#96;Valcre-HomeTab-*.md&#96; - HomeTab analysis (3 files)\n- &#96;Report-Builder-Field-Registry.md&#96; - Field registry documentation",

        "contents": "2. Read raw JSONL manually if needed\n\n💡 At the end of this session, use /session-summary to create passover file\n   for next time. It will auto-create .passover-session/ folder and use\n   unified naming: YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-# [topic].md\n\nReady. What would you like to work on?\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Key Benefits\n\n**vs Reading Raw JSONL Blindly:**\n- ✅ Focused instructions (not 2000 lines to parse without guidance)\n- ✅ Know exactly what matters\n- ✅ Faster startup (read what's important, not everything)\n- ✅ Human-curated context from previous agent\n\n**vs Manual Summaries:**\n- ✅ Executable instructions (not just informational text)\n- ✅ Points to source of truth (JSONL lines, files)\n- ✅ Includes what to test/verify\n- ✅ Warns about broken things\n\n---\n\n## Activation Protocol\n\n**When agent starts:**\n1. Run &#96;pwd&#96; (know location)\n2. **AUTO-TRIGGER THIS SKILL** (load passover)\n3. Execute passover instructions\n4. Present summary\n5. Ready\n\n**No asking, no permission - just load context and go.**\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Unified Session Management SOP\n\nThis skill is the executable component of the unified session startup system.\n\n**Works with:**\n- &#96;.passover-session/&#96; folder (where passover files live)\n- &#96;/session-summary&#96; command (creates passover files with unified naming)\n- Unified SOP: &#96;.passover-session/.sop-unified-session-management.md&#96;\n- Project README files (documents project setup)\n\n**Naming convention (Unified SOP):**\n- **Session summaries:** &#96;YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-# [topic].md&#96;\n- **Terminal pasovers:** &#96;YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-# terminal-[topic].md&#96;\n- **Prompt results:** &#96;YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-#-N terminal-result.md&#96;\n\n**Behavior:**\n- Generic for any agent (not agent-specific)\n- Works with any project (not project-specific)\n- Auto-activates at startup\n- Reports capabilities before loading context\n- Uses unified naming convention for all projects\n\n---\n\n## Where Passover Files Live\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./.passover-session/YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-# [topic].md\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Examples:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./.passover-session/25-11-02 | APR Ses-10.md\n./.passover-session/25-11-02 | APR Ses-10 systems-corrections.md",

        "contents": "## Activation Protocol\n\n**When agent starts:**\n1. Run &#96;pwd&#96; (know location)\n2. **AUTO-TRIGGER THIS SKILL** (load passover)\n3. Execute passover instructions\n4. Present summary\n5. Ready\n\n**No asking, no permission - just load context and go.**\n\n## Integration with Startup System\n\nThis skill is the executable component of the session startup system.\n\n**Works with:**\n- &#96;.passover-session/&#96; folder (where passover files live)\n- &#96;/session-summary&#96; command (creates passover files)\n- Project README files (documents project setup)\n\n**Behavior:**\n- Generic for any agent (not agent-specific)\n- Works with any project (not project-specific)\n- Auto-activates at startup\n- Reports capabilities before loading context\n\n## Where Passover Files Live\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./passover-session/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md  (in current project directory)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**NOT** &#96;.claude/sessions/&#96; (that's Claude Code's system)\n**NOT** &#96;~/.claude/sessions/&#96; (that's deprecated)\n\nClean, clear, separate from Claude Code's auto-generated files.\n\n\n## Assistant\n\nI'll execute the session startup protocol. Let me first navigate to your specified directory and check capabilities.\n\n**Tool Call: Navigate to APR System Roadmap directory**\nStatus: Completed\n\nTerminal:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v3/.APR-System-Roadmap\n\n\n\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n**Tool Call: Check running Docker containers**\nStatus: Completed\n\nTerminal:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nNAMES                 PORTS\ncool_yonath\nhappy_faraday\nquizzical_pasteur\neager_stonebraker\ncrazy_chatterjee\nsleepy_rosalind\npensive_mclean        3000/tcp\nsad_lichterman        3000/tcp\nmcp-n8n-cloud\nmcp-fast-filesystem\nopen-webui            0.0.0.0:3000-&gt;8080/tcp, [::]:3000-&gt;8080/tcp\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n**Tool Call: Check for passover session directory**\nStatus: Completed\n\nTerminal:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ntotal 128\ndrwxr-xr-x   9 bencrowe  staff    288 Oct 26 09:30 .\ndrwxr-xr-x@ 34 bencrowe  staff   1088 Nov  1 08:35 ..\n-rw-r--r--   1 bencrowe  staff   6369 Oct 24 20:20 2025-10-24-2020.md\n-rw-r--r--   1 bencrowe  staff   5746 Oct 24 21:38 2025-10-24-2048.md\n-rw-r--r--   1 bencrowe  staff  11585 Oct 25 10:26 2025-10-25-1024.md",

        "contents": "- ❌ Wrong naming convention\n\n---\n\n## Execution Order\n\n1. Determine naming (date, project, session #, topic)\n2. Analyze session (files, discoveries, blockers)\n3. Create passover file (treasure map)\n4. Write to &#96;.passover-session/YY-MM-DD | PROJECT Ses-# terminal-[topic].md&#96;\n5. Extract learnings (unless --skip-memory)\n6. Ingest to memory via webhook\n7. Display summary\n\n**The passover file is what matters most.**\nMemory ingestion is bonus.\n\n---\n\n**Integrated with:** Unified Session Management SOP\n**Location:** &#96;.passover-session/.sop-unified-session-management.md&#96;\n**Last Updated:** 2025-11-02\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n**Tool Call: Read session-startup skill**\nStatus: Completed\n\nTerminal:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n---\nname: session-startup\ndescription: Load session context from passover file and restore full agent capability. Auto-loads at startup to enable continuity across sessions.\ncategory: startup\nactivation: automatic\npriority: critical\n---\n\n# Session Startup - Context Loader\n\n**AUTOMATIC ACTIVATION:** This skill runs at agent startup (after &#96;pwd&#96;).\n\n## Activation Keywords and Triggers\n\n**Auto-Triggers (no user action needed):**\n- ✅ Agent startup sequence (runs after &#96;pwd&#96;)\n- ✅ Project directory change (cd to new project)\n- ✅ Session continuation (user re-engages after compression/handoff)\n\n**Explicit User Keywords (agent should recognize and trigger):**\n- &#96;\"load context\"&#96; - Load previous session context\n- &#96;\"session startup\"&#96; - Start session with full context restoration\n- &#96;\"continue session\"&#96; - Resume from previous work\n- &#96;\"load passover\"&#96; - Explicitly load passover file\n- &#96;\"restore context\"&#96; - Restore full capability from previous session\n- &#96;\"startup\"&#96; - Generic startup trigger\n- &#96;\"session load\"&#96; - Load session information\n\n**When to Trigger (Recognition Rules):**\n- User says any of the keywords above\n- Agent startup sequence completes (&#96;pwd&#96; executed)\n- Project directory confirmed in passover-session location\n- User message contains \"session\", \"context\", \"passover\", \"startup\", \"continue\", \"restore\", \"load\"\n\n**Agent Recognition Pattern:**\nIf user message contains ANY of these keywords → **Invoke session-startup skill immediately**",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "contents": "3. **JSONB storage for report data:** Store entire report state as JSONB in &#96;report_builder_data&#96; table. Rationale: Flexible schema, easy versioning, matches Zustand store shape.\n\n---\n\n## Micro to Macro Map\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMICRO: Image configurator component\n  |\nSERVES: image-mgt section (160 fields, 55 images)\n  |\nSERVES: Report Builder editor experience\n  |\nSERVES: Stage 4 completion\n  |\nMACRO: Complete client-to-report pipeline\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMICRO: Map Title default experience\n  |\nSERVES: Better UX for fresh templates\n  |\nSERVES: Report Builder usability\n  |\nSERVES: Stage 4 completion\n  |\nMACRO: Complete client-to-report pipeline\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Key Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| &#96;docs/APR-Hybrid-Execution-Plan.md&#96; | **Hybrid PRP execution plan for Phase 0-7** |\n| &#96;docs/bc research &amp; notes/FULL-ECOSYSTEM-INTEGRATION.md&#96; | Complete system architecture |\n| &#96;src/features/report-builder/schema/fieldRegistry.ts&#96; | 944 field definitions |\n| &#96;src/features/report-builder/store/reportBuilderStore.ts&#96; | Zustand store (6,340 lines) |\n| &#96;src/features/image-configurator/&#96; | Standalone image management feature |\n| &#96;docs/PENDING-TASKS.md&#96; | Current task list |\n| &#96;docs/TDD-PAGE-ARCHITECTURE.md&#96; | Page/section architecture |\n\n---\n\n## Session Log\n\n| Date | Focus | Outcome |\n|------|-------|---------|\n| 2025-12-31 | Ecosystem research | Created FULL-ECOSYSTEM-INTEGRATION.md |\n| 2026-01-03 | Image configurator | Built standalone at /image-test |\n| 2026-01-05 | Orchestrator setup | Created context + /continue command |\n| 2026-01-05 | Hybrid methodology | Created APR-Hybrid-Execution-Plan.md applying Hybrid PRP to Phases 0-7 |\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria (from FULL-ECOSYSTEM-INTEGRATION.md)\n\n- [ ] Home section visible as first section in sidebar\n- [ ] Home section has 8 subsections with ~40 fields\n- [ ] Job data pre-populates Home Tab from Job Detail\n- [ ] Report Builder auto-saves to Supabase (2s debounce)\n- [ ] Version history tracked per job\n- [ ] Conditions use dynamic list UI (add/remove items)\n- [ ] Locked fields in other sections show \"Edit in Home\" link",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "3. **JSONB storage for report data:** Store entire report state as JSONB in &#96;report_builder_data&#96; table. Rationale: Flexible schema, easy versioning, matches Zustand store shape.",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "# Registry &amp; Workbook Expert\n\n**Purpose:** Activate expertise for field registry management and Valcre workbook logic.\n\n---\n\n## Role\n\nYou are the **Registry &amp; Workbook Expert** - the specialist who understands:\n- How field IDs in our system map to Valcre workbook named ranges\n- The origin of all calculation logic (Direct Cap, Sales Comp, Cost Approach)\n- Field verification protocols before adding/modifying registry entries\n\n---\n\n## Persona Switching (IMPORTANT)\n\n**When NOT deploying subagents, switch to the appropriate persona yourself:**\n\n| Work Type | Switch To | Why |\n|-----------|-----------|-----|\n| Registry/Schema work | **typescript-pro** | Type-safe field definitions, schema patterns |\n| React components (EditPanel, PreviewPanel) | **react-specialist** | React 18+ hooks, performance, component patterns |\n| General frontend/UI | **frontend-developer** | UI engineering, web standards, accessibility |\n| Testing/Verification | **qa-expert** | Systematic testing, quality metrics |\n| Store/State management | **typescript-pro** | Zustand patterns, type safety |\n| Build/Deploy issues | **devops-engineer** | CI/CD, build configuration |\n\n**How to switch:** State your persona at the start of focused work:\n&gt; \"Switching to react-specialist for this EditPanel work...\"\n\n**Default for this domain:** **TypeScript-Pro** for registry and store work.\n\n---\n\n## Core Reference Files\n\n### 1. APR Domain Core Management Folder (YOUR WORKSPACE)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v3/docs/15-Contract-review/0-APR-Domain-Core-Mgt/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n**This is your dedicated workspace.** Contains:\n- &#96;APR-DOMAIN-KNOWLEDGE-25.12.26.md&#96; - **PRIMARY REFERENCE** - Field counts, calc engine status, valuation approaches\n- &#96;CALC-ENGINE-FIELD-MAP.md&#96; - Maps calc engine to field registry\n- &#96;VALCRE-TO-TEMPLATE-CROSSWALK.md&#96; - Valcre → Template field mapping\n- &#96;VALCRE_REPORT_STRUCTURE.md&#96; - 88-sheet workbook structure\n- &#96;VALCRE_TABLE_FIELD_MAPPING.md&#96; - Table-level field mappings\n- &#96;Valcre-HomeTab-*.md&#96; - HomeTab analysis (3 files)\n- &#96;Report-Builder-Field-Registry.md&#96; - Field registry documentation",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/02-Email-&amp;-Pipedrive-CRM/README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
